options.content:
**Layer 1 (ADR-010/ADR-011)** is the exact, PromQL-queryable keyframe layer: palimpsestd writes every recovered/keyframe value for a logical series to Prometheus via remote-write, tagged with the `palimpsest_logical_name` label (see `internal/adapters/remoteprom`). The panel below plots one series' exact value over time as **points, not an interpolated line** — deliberately, because the *density* of points is itself informative:

- During quiet windows, points land only at **keyframe cadence** (`keyframe_every` x `flush_interval` — a 60s grid at typical production settings; faster in this demo).
- During a detected deviation, `solveWindow` additionally writes the **recovered value every flush** (`flush_interval`, ~10s) for as long as that series stays in the recovered support set.

So a sparse 60s grid that suddenly densifies to ~10s points *is* the anomaly detail overlay — no separate query or annotation channel is needed, because both layers share the same exact-value wire path (ADR-010's Layer 1) and the same PromQL series. Pick a series with `$logical_name` above; one with an injected anomaly, drift, or a hot instance (see `demo/README.md`) shows the effect most clearly.

panels.title: Layer 1 exact value — $logical_name
panels.description: Raw points (no interpolation): sparse = keyframe-cadence only (quiet), dense = recovered-value writes during a detected deviation (ADR-009/ADR-010).

panels.title: Known series (label_values)
panels.description: Every palimpsest_logical_name Layer 1 has seen so far this session — pick one for the panel above.
